title: Flashcard Collection — Self Introduction (Day 2)
description: 500 flashcards covering vocabulary, phrases, grammar, and sentence patterns for Day 2: Self Introduction. Each card has a front (Hindi/question) and back (English answer with explanation).

cards.back: My name is ___.
cards.hint: Most formal way to state your name

I am interested in ___.

cards.front: मेरा लक्ष्य ___ है।

cards.back: Nice to meet you. / Pleased to meet you.

cards.front: मैं ___ सालों से इस क्षेत्र में हूँ।
cards.back: I have been in this field for ___ years.

cards.back: I have been working here for ___ years.

cards.front: मेरी मुख्य ताकत ___ है।
cards.back: My biggest strength is ___.

cards.front: मैं इस opportunity के लिए उत्साहित हूँ।
cards.back: I am excited about this opportunity.

cards.front: मैं आपकी team के साथ काम करने का इंतजार करूंगा।
cards.back: I look forward to working with your team.

cards.front: मुझे विश्वास है कि मेरी skills इस role के लिए suitable हैं।
cards.back: I believe my skills align perfectly with this role.

cards.front: मेरे परिवार में ___ लोग हैं।
cards.back: There are ___ people in my family. / My family has ___ members.
My father is a ___.
My mother is a ___.

cards.front: मुझे ___ से बहुत प्रेरणा मिलती है।
cards.back: I am deeply inspired by ___.

cards.front: मैं ___ में specialize करता/करती हूँ।
cards.back: I specialize in ___.

cards.front: मेरी पृष्ठभूमि ___ में है।
cards.back: My background is in ___.

cards.front: मैं ___ के रूप में अपनी पहचान रखता/रखती हूँ।
cards.back: I identify myself as a ___.

cards.front: मुझे इस meeting में आमंत्रित करने के लिए धन्यवाद।
cards.back: Thank you for having me / Thank you for this opportunity.

cards.front: अपने बारे में कुछ बताइए।

cards.front: क्या आप थोड़ा अपने बारे में बता सकते हैं?

cards.front: मैं freshly graduated हूँ।
cards.back: I am a fresh graduate.

cards.front: मुझे ___ साल का अनुभव है।
cards.back: I have ___ years of experience.

cards.front: मैं currently ___ में job ढूंढ रहा/रही हूँ।
cards.back: I am currently looking for opportunities in ___.

cards.front: मेरी expertise ___ में है।
cards.back: My expertise lies in ___.

cards.front: मैं ___ में पहला/पहली हूँ।
cards.back: I ranked first in ___. / I was the top student in ___.

cards.front: मेरी उपलब्धियों में ___ शामिल है।
cards.back: My achievements include ___.

cards.front: मैं तेज़ी से सीखने वाला/वाली हूँ।
cards.back: I am a quick learner.

cards.front: मैं team में काम करने में विश्वास रखता/रखती हूँ।
cards.back: I am a team player. / I believe in teamwork.

cards.front: मैं pressure में अच्छा काम करता/करती हूँ।
cards.back: I work well under pressure.

cards.front: मैं detail-oriented हूँ।
cards.back: I am detail-oriented. / I pay great attention to detail.

cards.front: मैं एक self-motivated person हूँ।
cards.back: I am a self-motivated individual.

cards.front: मैं हमेशा नया सीखने के लिए उत्सुक रहता/रहती हूँ।
cards.back: I am always eager to learn new things.

cards.front: मेरी communication skills अच्छी हैं।
cards.back: I have strong communication skills.

cards.front: मैं बदलाव के साथ आसानी से adjust कर लेता/लेती हूँ।
cards.back: I am adaptable to change. / I adapt easily to new environments.

cards.front: मेरी सबसे बड़ी achievement ___ है।
cards.back: My greatest achievement is ___.

cards.front: मैं multilingual हूँ।
cards.back: I am multilingual. / I speak ___ languages.

cards.front: मैं ___ में fluent हूँ।
cards.back: I am fluent in ___.

cards.front: मुझे ___ में basic knowledge है।
cards.back: I have a basic knowledge of ___.

cards.front: मैं अपनी skills को और improve करना चाहता/चाहती हूँ।
cards.back: I am looking to further improve my skills.

cards.front: मेरा सपना ___ बनना है।
cards.back: My dream is to become ___.

cards.front: मैं अपने काम के प्रति प्रतिबद्ध हूँ।
cards.back: I am committed to my work. / I am dedicated to my profession.

cards.front: मुझे नई challenges का सामना करना पसंद है।
cards.back: I enjoy facing new challenges.

cards.front: मेरी सबसे बड़ी कमज़ोरी यह है कि मैं काम में बहुत perfectionist हूँ।
cards.back: My biggest weakness is that I tend to be a perfectionist.

cards.front: मुझे यहाँ आकर बहुत खुशी हुई।
cards.back: I am delighted to be here. / I am thrilled to join this team.

cards.front: Name introduce karne ka sabse formal tarika kya hai?
cards.back: My name is [Full Name].

cards.front: Birth place batane ke liye kaunsa tense use hota hai?
cards.back: Past Simple: I WAS born in ___.

cards.front: Company mein kitne saalon se kaam kar rahe hain — ye batane ke liye kaunsa tense?
cards.back: Present Perfect Continuous: I have BEEN WORKING at ___ FOR ___ years.

cards.front: Specific company ke liye kaunsi preposition? (Google, TCS, etc.)
cards.back: AT or FOR: I work AT Google / I work FOR Google

cards.front: Industry/sector ke liye kaunsi preposition? (IT, finance, etc.)
cards.back: IN: I work IN the IT sector / I work IN finance

cards.front: 'For' aur 'Since' mein kya fark hai?
cards.back: FOR + duration (3 years, 6 months). SINCE + specific point (since 2020, since January).
cards.hint: FOR = how long. SINCE = starting point.

cards.front: Stative verbs ke examples kya hain jo -ing nahi lete?
cards.back: like, love, hate, know, believe, understand, want, need, prefer, remember, seem, appear

cards.front: 'Interested' ke baad kaunsi preposition aati hai?
cards.back: IN: I am interested IN music.

cards.front: Hobby batane ke liye 'enjoy/love/like' ke baad kya aata hai?
cards.back: Gerund (-ing form): I enjoy READING / I love PLAYING / I like COOKING

cards.front: Goal batane ke liye kya structure use hota hai?
cards.back: My goal is TO + base verb: My goal is TO BECOME an engineer.

cards.front: 'An' kab use hota hai?
cards.back: Before vowel SOUNDS (A, E, I, O, U sounds). Based on pronunciation, not spelling.
cards.hint: an engineer, an MBA, an IT professional, an hour

cards.front: 'A' kab use hota hai?
cards.back: Before consonant SOUNDS. Based on pronunciation.
cards.hint: a doctor, a university (Y sound), a European (Y sound)

cards.front: Education complete hone ke baad kaunsa tense use hota hai?
cards.back: Past Simple: I COMPLETED my B.Tech in 2022.
cards.hint: Completed = specific past time = past simple

cards.front: 'Fond of' ke baad kya aata hai?
cards.back: Gerund: I am fond of READING / TRAVELLING / COOKING

cards.front: Family mein kitne log hain — 2 tarike mein kaise batate hain?
cards.back: 1. My family HAS ___ members. 2. There ARE ___ people in my family.

cards.front: Subject-verb agreement: 'My hobbies ___' (is/are)?
cards.back: My hobbies ARE reading and cooking. (plural subject → ARE)

cards.front: Subject-verb agreement: 'My hobby ___' (is/are)?
cards.back: My hobby IS reading. (singular subject → IS)

cards.front: 'Myself Rahul' kyun galat hai?
cards.back: Myself is a reflexive pronoun — used for emphasis or reflection, not for direct introduction. Say 'My name is Rahul' or 'I am Rahul'.

cards.front: 'I am working since 3 years' mein kya galat hai?
cards.back: TWO errors: 1. Use 'have been working' (not 'am working') for ongoing duration. 2. Use 'for' (not 'since') with a period. CORRECT: I have been working for 3 years.

cards.front: 'I have joined in 2022' mein kya galat hai?
cards.back: Specific past time (2022) = Simple Past only. CORRECT: I JOINED in 2022.
cards.hint: With time words (in 2022, last year): use Simple Past

cards.front: 'Passed out from college' ka correct version kya hai?
cards.back: I graduated from college. ('Passed out' = fainted in standard English!)
cards.hint: Indian English 'passed out' = graduated. Standard English: graduated from.

cards.front: 'I am doing job' ka correct version kya hai?
cards.back: I work at ___ . / I am working at ___.

cards.front: City names ke pehle 'the' lagate hain ya nahi?
cards.back: No 'the' before city names: Delhi, Mumbai, London (not 'the Delhi'). Exception: the USA, the UK, the Netherlands.

cards.front: '2 years back' ka correct version kya hai?
cards.back: 2 years AGO. (Not 'back' — 'back' is informal/Indian English)

cards.front: 'I am having a wife' mein kya galat hai?
cards.back: TWO errors: 1. 'am having' for possession is wrong — use 'have'. 2. No additional error. CORRECT: I have a wife.

cards.front: 'Childrens' kya galat hai?
cards.back: Yes! 'Children' is already plural. Never add 's' to it. child → children (irregular plural).

cards.front: 'Very much excited' kyun galat hai?
cards.back: 'Very much' is used before verbs, not adjectives. Correct: 'very excited' or 'so excited'. 'Very much' example: 'I appreciate it very much' (verb).

cards.front: Relative clause mein 'which' kab use hota hai?
cards.back: 'Which' is used for things/non-persons. 'Who' is used for people. 'That' can be used for both in restrictive clauses.
cards.hint: who = person | which = thing | that = either (restrictive)

cards.front: Gerund kya hota hai? Example dijiye.
cards.back: A gerund is a verb + -ing used as a noun. Examples: reading, cooking, playing. 'I enjoy READING' — here READING is a gerund.

cards.front: Infinitive kya hota hai? Example dijiye.
cards.back: Infinitive = to + base verb. Examples: to read, to cook, to play. 'My goal is TO BECOME a doctor' — to become = infinitive.

cards.front: Present Perfect Continuous ka formula kya hai?
cards.back: Subject + have/has + been + verb+ing. Example: I have been working here for 2 years.

cards.front: Simple Past ka formula kya hai?
cards.back: Subject + verb(past form). Regular: work → worked. Irregular: go → went, complete → completed.
cards.hint: verb + -ed for regular verbs. Irregular forms must be memorized.

cards.front: Present Perfect ka formula kya hai?
cards.back: Subject + have/has + past participle. Example: I have completed my degree. She has worked here for a year.

cards.front: Passive voice ka use kab hota hai introduction mein?
cards.back: When the action is more important than who did it. Example: 'I was promoted to senior role' (passive — more formal). 'My manager promoted me' (active).

cards.front: Condition mein 'if' ke baad kaunsa tense?
cards.back: For real/possible conditions: If + Simple Present... will + base verb. Example: 'If you have any questions, I will be happy to answer them.'
cards.hint: If + present, will + base verb (first conditional)

cards.front: 'I am belong to Delhi' mein kya galat hai?
cards.back: 'Belong' is the main verb here — no need for 'am' before it. CORRECT: I belong to Delhi. OR: I am from Delhi.

cards.front: Apni language skills batane ka sahi tarika kya hai?
cards.back: I am fluent in Hindi and English. / I can speak both Hindi and English. / I have working knowledge of French.
cards.hint: Fluent in / can speak / working knowledge of

cards.front: Formal letter/email mein greeting kaise dete hain?
cards.back: Dear Mr./Ms. [Surname], / Dear [First Name], / To Whom It May Concern (if recipient unknown)
cards.hint: Formal: Dear + title + surname. Semi-formal: Dear + first name.

cards.front: Formal closing kaise karte hain?
cards.back: Yours sincerely (when you know the name) / Yours faithfully (when recipient unknown) / Warm regards / Best regards
cards.hint: Sincerely = name known. Faithfully = name unknown.

cards.front: Compound adjective kya hota hai? Example dijiye.
cards.back: A compound adjective is two or more words used together to describe a noun, usually hyphenated. Examples: well-organized, fast-paced, data-driven, result-oriented, English-speaking.
cards.hint: Hyphenated adjectives before a noun: a well-organized team

cards.front: Apni graduation year batane ka sahi tarika?
cards.back: I graduated from ___ in [year]. / I completed my ___ from ___ in [year].

cards.front: Apni internship batane ka sahi tarika?
cards.back: I completed a [duration] internship at [Company] / I interned at [Company] for [duration].

cards.front: Apna percentage/CGPA batane ka sahi tarika?
cards.back: I scored ___% in my board exams. / I graduated with a CGPA of ___. / I completed my degree with first-class distinction.
cards.hint: scored + % | CGPA of + number | with + distinction

cards.front: Formal mein 'I am happy to meet you' se better kya hai?
cards.back: It is a pleasure to meet you. / I am delighted to meet you. / Pleased to meet you.

cards.front: Online meeting mein apna location kaise batate hain?
cards.back: I am based in [City]. / I am joining from [City]. / I am calling from [City].
cards.hint: based in / joining from / calling from

cards.front: Apna number of years ka experience batane ke 2 tarike?
cards.back: 1. I have [X] years of experience in ___. 2. I bring [X] years of experience to the table.
cards.hint: have X years OR bring X years to the table

cards.front: Phone pe apna introduction kaise dete hain?
cards.back: Good morning! My name is ___. I am calling from ___. / This is ___ speaking. / Am I speaking with ___?
cards.hint: Phone: 'This is [Name] speaking' or 'My name is [Name], calling from'

cards.front: Social media pe casual introduction ka opening kya hai?
cards.back: Hi! / Hey everyone! / Hello! I am [Name]. For those who don't know me — I am ___.
cards.hint: Casual, friendly opening for social media

cards.front: Conference ya public speaking mein introduction ka opening kya hai?
cards.back: Good morning/afternoon, distinguished guests / esteemed colleagues / everyone. It is an honour/pleasure to be here today.

cards.front: Email introduction ka subject line kaisa hota hai?
cards.back: Introduction — [Your Name], [Your Designation] / Hello from [Your Name] / [Your Name] — New [Team Name] Member
cards.hint: Clear, concise subject with name and role

categories.name: Vocabulary — Words Related to Self Introduction

cards.front: introduce
cards.back: परिचय देना (to present yourself or others for the first time)
cards.example: Please introduce yourself to the team.

cards.front: introduction
cards.back: परिचय (the act of presenting yourself)
cards.example: Give a brief introduction of yourself.

cards.front: profession
cards.back: पेशा (your job or career)
cards.example: My profession is teaching.

cards.front: occupation
cards.back: व्यवसाय (the job or work you do)
cards.example: What is your occupation?

cards.front: designation
cards.back: पदनाम (your official job title)
cards.example: My designation is Senior Manager.

cards.front: experience
cards.back: अनुभव (knowledge or skill gained over time)
cards.example: I have 5 years of experience in marketing.

cards.front: qualification
cards.back: योग्यता (your educational or professional credentials)
cards.example: My highest qualification is an MBA.

cards.front: achievement
cards.back: उपलब्धि (something you have accomplished)
cards.example: My greatest achievement is leading a team of 20 people.

cards.front: confident
cards.back: आत्मविश्वासी (sure of yourself)
cards.example: Give a confident introduction.

cards.front: passionate
cards.back: उत्साही / जोशीला (deeply interested and enthusiastic)
cards.example: I am passionate about technology.

cards.front: specialize
cards.back: विशेषज्ञता रखना (to focus on one particular area)
cards.example: I specialize in data analytics.

cards.front: enthusiasm
cards.back: उत्साह (great excitement and interest)
cards.example: Show enthusiasm during your introduction.

cards.front: versatile
cards.back: बहुमुखी (able to do many different things)
cards.example: I am a versatile professional.

cards.front: proficient
cards.back: निपुण / कुशल (skilled and competent)
cards.example: I am proficient in three languages.

cards.front: aspiration
cards.back: आकांक्षा / लक्ष्य (a strong desire to achieve something)
cards.example: My aspiration is to become a CEO.

cards.front: background
cards.back: पृष्ठभूमि (your history, education, or experience)
cards.example: I have a background in finance.

cards.front: expertise
cards.back: विशेषज्ञता (special skill or knowledge)
cards.example: My expertise lies in cloud computing.

cards.front: diligent
cards.back: परिश्रमी / मेहनती (hardworking and careful)
cards.example: I am a diligent worker.

cards.front: resilient
cards.back: लचीला / मज़बूत (able to recover from difficulties)
cards.example: I am resilient and handle pressure well.

cards.front: collaborate
cards.back: सहयोग करना (to work together with others)
cards.example: I enjoy collaborating with diverse teams.

cards.front: innovative
cards.back: नवाचारी (introducing new ideas; creative)
cards.example: I am an innovative thinker.

cards.front: articulate
cards.back: स्पष्ट रूप से बोलने वाला (able to express yourself clearly)
cards.example: She is very articulate in her speech.

cards.front: organized
cards.back: व्यवस्थित (arranged in a neat and efficient way)
cards.example: I am highly organized and punctual.

cards.front: committed
cards.back: समर्पित (dedicated to a cause or task)
cards.example: I am fully committed to my work.

cards.front: ambitious
cards.back: महत्वाकांक्षी (having strong desire to succeed)
cards.example: I am ambitious and goal-oriented.

cards.front: punctual
cards.back: समय का पाबंद (always on time)
cards.example: I am always punctual.

cards.front: adaptable
cards.back: अनुकूलनीय (able to adjust to new situations)
cards.example: I am highly adaptable to change.

cards.front: leadership
cards.back: नेतृत्व (ability to guide or lead others)
cards.example: I have strong leadership qualities.

cards.front: analytical
cards.back: विश्लेषणात्मक (good at examining things carefully)
cards.example: I have an analytical mindset.

cards.front: empathetic
cards.back: सहानुभूतिपूर्ण (able to understand others' feelings)
cards.example: I am an empathetic team leader.

cards.front: networking
cards.back: लोगों से संबंध बनाना (building professional relationships)
cards.example: I enjoy networking with professionals.

cards.front: portfolio
cards.back: कार्य संग्रह (a collection of your work)
cards.example: I have a portfolio of 30+ projects.

cards.front: mentorship
cards.back: मार्गदर्शन (guidance from a more experienced person)
cards.example: I believe in mentorship and learning from seniors.

cards.front: initiative
cards.back: पहल (taking action without being told)
cards.example: I take initiative in every project.

cards.front: accountability
cards.back: जवाबदेही (responsibility for your actions)
cards.example: I believe in accountability in all my work.

cards.front: dedication
cards.back: समर्पण (strong commitment to something)
cards.example: My success comes from dedication and hard work.

cards.front: eloquent
cards.back: प्रभावशाली वक्ता (speaking fluently and persuasively)
cards.example: She is an eloquent speaker.

cards.front: approachable
cards.back: मिलनसार (easy to talk to)
cards.example: I am approachable and open to feedback.

cards.front: perseverance
cards.back: दृढ़ता (continuing despite difficulties)
cards.example: My greatest quality is perseverance.

cards.front: integrity
cards.back: ईमानदारी / सत्यनिष्ठा (being honest and having moral principles)
cards.example: I always work with integrity.

cards.front: colleague
cards.back: सहकर्मी (a person you work with)
cards.example: My colleagues describe me as hardworking.

cards.front: mentor
cards.back: मार्गदर्शक (an experienced advisor)
cards.example: My mentor helped me grow in my career.

cards.front: intern
cards.back: प्रशिक्षु (a trainee doing work experience)
cards.example: I worked as an intern for 6 months.

cards.front: freelancer
cards.back: स्वतंत्र पेशेवर (someone who works independently for various clients)
cards.example: I work as a freelancer in graphic design.

cards.front: entrepreneur
cards.back: उद्यमी (someone who starts their own business)
cards.example: I am an entrepreneur with two startups.

cards.front: tenure
cards.back: कार्यकाल (the period of time you hold a job)
cards.example: During my tenure at ABC Company, I led 5 projects.

cards.front: curriculum vitae (CV)
cards.back: पाठ्यचर्या (a detailed document of your qualifications and experience)
cards.example: I have attached my CV for your reference.

cards.front: resume
cards.back: संक्षिप्त जीवन परिचय (a brief document summarizing your skills and experience)
cards.example: My resume highlights all my achievements.

cards.front: referral
cards.back: अनुशंसा (a recommendation from someone who knows you)
cards.example: I got this job through a referral from a colleague.

cards.front: testimonial
cards.back: प्रशंसापत्र (a statement about someone's qualities)
cards.example: My manager gave me a glowing testimonial.

cards.front: accomplishment
cards.back: उपलब्धि (something you have achieved successfully)
cards.example: Tell us about your greatest accomplishment.

cards.front: contribution
cards.back: योगदान (something you give or add to a group or effort)
cards.example: I made significant contributions to the project.

cards.front: milestone
cards.back: महत्वपूर्ण पड़ाव (a significant achievement in your career/life)
cards.example: Getting promoted was a major milestone for me.

cards.front: breakthrough
cards.back: सफलता की कुंजी (a sudden major achievement)
cards.example: My breakthrough came when I landed my first big client.

cards.front: transition
cards.back: बदलाव / संक्रमण (change from one state/role to another)
cards.example: I made a smooth transition from engineering to management.

cards.front: aspire
cards.back: आकांक्षा रखना (to have a strong desire to achieve)
cards.example: I aspire to lead a company one day.

cards.front: pursue
cards.back: पीछे जाना / अनुसरण करना (to follow or work towards a goal)
cards.example: I pursued a career in digital marketing.

cards.front: embark
cards.back: शुरुआत करना (to begin a new venture or journey)
cards.example: I am excited to embark on this new role.

cards.front: leverage
cards.back: उपयोग करना / लाभ उठाना (to use something to your advantage)
cards.example: I leverage my technical skills to solve business problems.

cards.front: impact
cards.back: प्रभाव (a strong effect on something)
cards.example: I want to make a positive impact through my work.

cards.front: passion
cards.back: जुनून / जोश (a very strong feeling of enthusiasm)
cards.example: Teaching is my passion.

cards.front: vision
cards.back: दृष्टि / सपना (a clear idea of what you want to achieve)
cards.example: My vision is to build a sustainable business.

cards.front: mission
cards.back: लक्ष्य / उद्देश्य (your main purpose or goal)
cards.example: My mission is to improve education.

cards.front: values
cards.back: मूल्य (principles and beliefs that guide you)
cards.example: My core values are honesty, hard work, and respect.

cards.front: mindset
cards.back: मानसिकता (way of thinking)
cards.example: I have a growth mindset.

cards.front: strength
cards.back: ताकत / बल (a quality or ability that makes you good at something)
cards.example: My strength is problem-solving.

cards.front: weakness
cards.back: कमज़ोरी (an area where you are not as strong)
cards.example: My weakness is that I sometimes overwork.

cards.front: challenge
cards.back: चुनौती (a difficult situation that tests your ability)
cards.example: Every challenge makes me stronger.

cards.front: opportunity
cards.back: अवसर (a chance to do or achieve something)
cards.example: Thank you for this opportunity.

cards.front: growth
cards.back: विकास (development or improvement)
cards.example: I am focused on personal and professional growth.

cards.front: goal
cards.back: लक्ष्य (something you want to achieve)
cards.example: My short-term goal is to clear CA exams.

cards.front: objective
cards.back: उद्देश्य (a specific target you are working towards)
cards.example: My objective is to improve sales by 20%.

cards.front: career
cards.back: करियर (your professional life or working life)
cards.example: I want to build a successful career in finance.

cards.front: discipline
cards.back: अनुशासन (the ability to control yourself and work consistently)
cards.example: Success requires discipline and consistency.

cards.front: consistency
cards.back: निरंतरता (doing something regularly without stopping)
cards.example: I believe in consistency over motivation.

cards.front: responsible
cards.back: ज़िम्मेदार (able to be trusted to do what is right)
cards.example: I am a responsible and reliable employee.

cards.front: reliable
cards.back: भरोसेमंद (dependable; can be counted on)
cards.example: My team describes me as reliable.

cards.front: proactive
cards.back: पहल करने वाला (taking action before a problem occurs)
cards.example: I am a proactive problem-solver.

cards.front: strategic
cards.back: रणनीतिक (relating to a plan designed to achieve a goal)
cards.example: I think strategically about every project.

cards.front: credential
cards.back: प्रमाण-पत्र (a qualification or achievement that proves your ability)
cards.example: I have strong academic credentials.

cards.front: internship
cards.back: इंटर्नशिप (a temporary job to gain work experience)
cards.example: I completed my internship at Google.

cards.front: certificate
cards.back: प्रमाण-पत्र (an official document showing you completed something)
cards.example: I hold a certificate in digital marketing.

cards.front: graduate
cards.back: स्नातक / स्नातक होना (person who completed a degree OR to complete a degree)
cards.example: I graduated from Delhi University in 2022.

cards.front: undergraduate
cards.back: स्नातक (a student who has not yet completed a degree)
cards.example: I am an undergraduate student.

cards.front: postgraduate
cards.back: स्नातकोत्तर (having completed a Master's or higher degree)
cards.example: I am a postgraduate in Business Administration.

cards.front: distinction
cards.back: प्रथम श्रेणी से (highest grade level in graduation)
cards.example: I graduated with distinction.

cards.front: scholarship
cards.back: छात्रवृत्ति (money awarded to a student based on merit)
cards.example: I received a merit scholarship for two years.

cards.front: placement
cards.back: नौकरी प्रदान करना (getting a job after college, especially via campus recruitment)
cards.example: I got my first job through campus placement.

cards.front: promotion
cards.back: पदोन्नति (being given a higher rank or position)
cards.example: I received a promotion after two years.

cards.front: appraisal
cards.back: मूल्यांकन (a formal assessment of your performance at work)
cards.example: My appraisal rating is Excellent.

cards.front: transfer
cards.back: स्थानांतरण (moving to a different location or department)
cards.example: I was transferred to the Mumbai office.

cards.front: resignation
cards.back: इस्तीफा (formally leaving your job)
cards.example: I submitted my resignation with two weeks' notice.

cards.front: notice period
cards.back: नोटिस अवधि (time between resignation and last working day)
cards.example: I have a one-month notice period.

cards.front: joining date
cards.back: नियुक्ति तिथि (the date you will start a new job)
cards.example: My joining date is the 15th of next month.

cards.front: salary
cards.back: वेतन (money you receive for your work)
cards.example: I am looking for a competitive salary.

cards.front: compensation
cards.back: मुआवज़ा / वेतन-भत्ते (pay and benefits received for work)
cards.example: The compensation package includes salary and benefits.

cards.front: benefits
cards.back: लाभ / सुविधाएं (additional perks like health insurance, leave, etc.)
cards.example: The job comes with great benefits.

cards.front: remote work
cards.back: घर से काम (working from home or a location outside the office)
cards.example: I have been doing remote work for 2 years.

cards.front: hybrid
cards.back: मिश्रित (combination of office and remote work)
cards.example: We follow a hybrid work model.

cards.front: freelance
cards.back: स्वतंत्र रूप से काम करना (working independently for multiple clients)
cards.example: I do freelance graphic design on weekends.

categories.name: Sentence Patterns — Fill in the Blank

cards.front: My name ___ Rahul. (is/are/am)
cards.back: My name IS Rahul.

cards.front: I ___ from Mumbai. (am/is/are)
cards.back: I AM from Mumbai.

cards.front: I ___ born in Kolkata. (am/was/were)
cards.back: I WAS born in Kolkata.

cards.front: I work ___ TCS. (in/at/on)
cards.back: I work AT TCS.

cards.front: I work ___ the IT sector. (in/at/for)
cards.back: I work IN the IT sector.

cards.front: I have been working here ___ two years. (since/for/from)
cards.back: I have been working here FOR two years.

cards.front: I have been working here ___ 2022. (since/for/from)
cards.back: I have been working here SINCE 2022.

cards.front: I ___ in Mumbai for the past 3 years. (live/am living/have been living)
cards.back: I HAVE BEEN LIVING in Mumbai for the past 3 years.
cards.hint: Ongoing state from past → Present Perfect Continuous

cards.front: I am interested ___ music. (to/in/at)
cards.back: I am interested IN music.

cards.front: I am fond ___ reading. (to/in/of)
cards.back: I am fond OF reading.

cards.front: I enjoy ___ cricket. (play/playing/to play)
cards.back: I enjoy PLAYING cricket.

cards.front: My goal is ___ become an engineer. (to/for/of)
cards.back: My goal is TO become an engineer.

cards.front: My hobbies ___ reading and cooking. (is/are/am)
cards.back: My hobbies ARE reading and cooking.

cards.front: My hobby ___ playing cricket. (is/are/am)
cards.back: My hobby IS playing cricket.

cards.front: I completed my B.Tech ___ DTU ___ 2022. (from/in or from/at)
cards.back: I completed my B.Tech FROM DTU IN 2022.

cards.front: I ___ working here for 5 years. (am/was/have been)
cards.back: I HAVE BEEN working here for 5 years.

cards.front: I belong ___ Rajasthan. (from/to/in)
cards.back: I belong TO Rajasthan.

cards.front: I ___ graduated from IIT in 2021. (am/was/have)
cards.back: I graduated FROM IIT in 2021. (No 'have' — specific year = Simple Past)

cards.front: I am ___ engineer. (a/an/the)
cards.back: I am AN engineer.
cards.hint: Engineer starts with vowel sound 'E' → AN

cards.front: I am ___ software engineer. (a/an/the)
cards.back: I am A software engineer.
cards.hint: Software starts with consonant sound 'S' → A

cards.front: I am ___ IT professional. (a/an)
cards.back: I am AN IT professional.
cards.hint: IT = 'Eye-Tee' → starts with vowel 'I' sound → AN

cards.front: I joined this company 2 years ___ . (before/back/ago)
cards.back: I joined this company 2 years AGO.
cards.hint: Standard English for past time → AGO

cards.front: I have been ___ in this field for 4 years. (work/working/worked)
cards.back: I have been WORKING in this field for 4 years.

cards.front: I am ___ about technology. (passionate/passion/passionately)
cards.back: I am PASSIONATE about technology.

cards.front: I ___ married for 3 years. (am/was/have been)
cards.back: I HAVE BEEN married for 3 years.
cards.hint: Ongoing state from past → have been

cards.front: I specialize ___ data analytics. (at/in/of)
cards.back: I specialize IN data analytics.

cards.front: Nice to meet ___. (your/you/yours)
cards.back: Nice to meet YOU.

cards.front: I look forward to ___ with your team. (work/working/worked)
cards.back: I look forward to WORKING with your team.

cards.front: I am open ___ new opportunities. (to/for/at)
cards.back: I am open TO new opportunities.

cards.front: I am ___ in HR and management. (experienced/experience/experiencing)
cards.back: I am EXPERIENCED in HR and management.

cards.front: I ___ my degree from Delhi University. (complete/completed/completing)
cards.back: I COMPLETED my degree from Delhi University.

cards.front: I am ___ at problem-solving. (good/well/best)
cards.back: I am GOOD at problem-solving.

cards.front: I am proficient ___ Python and Java. (at/in/with)
cards.back: I am proficient IN Python and Java.

cards.front: I am fluent ___ English and Hindi. (in/at/on)
cards.back: I am fluent IN English and Hindi.

cards.front: My family has ___ members. (four/fours/foures)
cards.back: My family has FOUR members.

cards.front: There ___ five people in my family. (is/are/am)
cards.back: There ARE five people in my family.

cards.front: My father ___ a doctor. (is/are/am)
cards.back: My father IS a doctor.

cards.front: I have two ___. (childs/childrens/children)
cards.back: I have two CHILDREN.

cards.front: I ___ to become a manager one day. (want/am wanting/wants)
cards.back: I WANT to become a manager one day.

cards.front: It is ___ pleasure to meet you. (a/an/the)
cards.back: It is A pleasure to meet you.
cards.hint: Pleasure starts with consonant 'p' sound → A

cards.front: I am ___ to work in your organization. (exciting/excited/excite)
cards.back: I am EXCITED to work in your organization.
cards.hint: Person = excited. Thing = exciting. ('I' = person → excited)

cards.front: This opportunity is very ___. (exciting/excited/excite)
cards.back: This opportunity is very EXCITING.
cards.hint: Thing = exciting. Person = excited. ('opportunity' = thing → exciting)

cards.front: I am ___ in this work. (interest/interesting/interested)
cards.back: I am INTERESTED in this work.
cards.hint: Person feels → interested. Work makes you feel → interesting.

cards.front: This project is very ___. (interesting/interested/interest)
cards.back: This project is very INTERESTING.

cards.front: I have ___ my B.Tech with 8.5 CGPA. (complete/completed/completing)
cards.back: I have COMPLETED my B.Tech with 8.5 CGPA.

cards.front: I ___ the company in 2020. (joined/have joined/joining)
cards.back: I JOINED the company in 2020.

cards.front: I ___ working here recently. (started/have started/start)
cards.back: I HAVE STARTED working here recently. OR I started working here recently.
cards.hint: Both possible. 'Recently' works with both Present Perfect and Simple Past.

cards.front: I am ___ to hear from you soon. (hoping/hope/hoped)
cards.back: I am HOPING to hear from you soon.
cards.hint: Present Continuous 'am hoping' for current expectation

cards.front: I was ___ at the ceremony for my achievement. (honour/honoured/honouring)
cards.back: I was HONOURED at the ceremony.

cards.front: I am ___ by great leaders like APJ Abdul Kalam. (inspiring/inspired/inspiration)
cards.back: I am INSPIRED by great leaders.
cards.hint: Person is inspired. Leader is inspiring.

cards.front: My ___ is to become a doctor. (goal/goals/goaled)
cards.back: My GOAL is to become a doctor.

cards.front: I ___ my engineering with 85%. (scored/score/scoring)
cards.back: I scored 85% in my engineering. (Simple Past)

cards.front: I have been ___ for the past 6 months. (preparing/prepared/prepare)
cards.back: I have been PREPARING for the past 6 months.

cards.front: I am currently ___ my MBA. (pursue/pursuing/pursued)
cards.back: I am currently PURSUING my MBA.
cards.hint: Present Continuous for current action: am + -ing

cards.front: I ___ to this city three months ago. (move/moved/am moving)
cards.back: I MOVED to this city three months ago.
cards.hint: Specific time (three months ago) → Simple Past

cards.front: I am currently ___ for a new job. (look/looking/looked)
cards.back: I am currently LOOKING for a new job.

cards.front: I am ___ to join your team as soon as possible. (available/availability/availably)
cards.back: I am AVAILABLE to join your team.

cards.front: I believe ___ hard work leads to success. (which/that/what)
cards.back: I believe THAT hard work leads to success.

cards.front: I am a quick ___. (learn/learner/learning)
cards.back: I am a quick LEARNER.

cards.front: I work ___ under pressure. (good/well/best)
cards.back: I work WELL under pressure.

cards.front: I am ___ to meeting everyone. (look forward/looking forward/looked forward)
cards.back: I am LOOKING FORWARD to meeting everyone.
cards.hint: Present Continuous: am + looking forward to

cards.front: I ___ Delhi University. (graduated/graduated from/graduate)
cards.back: I graduated FROM Delhi University.

cards.front: I have a degree ___ Computer Science. (at/in/of)
cards.back: I have a degree IN Computer Science.

cards.front: I am ___ for the position of Marketing Manager. (apply/applying/applied)
cards.back: I am APPLYING for the position of Marketing Manager.

cards.front: Please ___ me the opportunity to prove myself. (give/gave/giving)
cards.back: Please GIVE me the opportunity to prove myself.

cards.front: I am ___ that I can handle this role efficiently. (confident/confidence/confide)
cards.back: I am CONFIDENT that I can handle this role.

cards.front: I was ___ in Kanpur but I grew up in Delhi. (born/birth/borning)
cards.back: I was BORN in Kanpur but I grew up in Delhi.

cards.front: I ___ the entire South India project single-handedly. (manage/managed/managing)
cards.back: I MANAGED the entire South India project single-handedly.

cards.front: I ___ for 10 years in the pharmaceutical industry. (worked/have worked/am working)
cards.back: I HAVE WORKED for 10 years in the pharmaceutical industry. OR I WORKED for 10 years...
cards.hint: Both possible. Present Perfect implies it's still relevant. Simple Past for completed.

cards.front: The project ___ me to learn new technologies. (helped/help/helping)
cards.back: The project HELPED me to learn new technologies.

cards.front: I am ___ to work in an environment that values innovation. (eager/eagerness/eagerly)
cards.back: I am EAGER to work in an environment that values innovation.

cards.front: I successfully ___ the sales target by 30%. (exceed/exceeded/exceeding)
cards.back: I successfully EXCEEDED the sales target by 30%.

cards.front: I would like to ___ myself to your team. (introduce/introducing/introduced)
cards.back: I would like to INTRODUCE myself to your team.

cards.front: I am ___ for joining any time after the 15th. (available/ability/availability)
cards.back: I am AVAILABLE for joining any time after the 15th.

cards.front: I am a self-___ person who does not need constant supervision. (motivate/motivated/motivation)
cards.back: I am a self-MOTIVATED person.

cards.front: I am ___ driven. (result/results/resulting)
cards.back: I am RESULT-driven. (result-driven — compound adjective)

cards.front: I have strong ___ skills. (communicate/communication/communicating)
cards.back: I have strong COMMUNICATION skills.
cards.hint: Noun form used as adjective: communication + skills

cards.front: I hold a ___ degree in Finance. (master/master's/masters)
cards.back: I hold a MASTER'S degree in Finance.
cards.hint: Possessive: Master's (the master's degree, belonging to the master level)

cards.front: I completed my ___ from IIT. (bachelor/bachelor's/bachelors)
cards.back: I completed my BACHELOR'S from IIT.

cards.front: I am seeking a challenging ___ where I can grow. (roll/role/roles)
cards.back: I am seeking a challenging ROLE where I can grow.
cards.hint: ROLE = position/responsibility. ROLL = bread roll or to roll.

cards.front: मैं problem को challenge के रूप में लेता हूँ।

cards.front: मैं currently MBA कर रहा हूँ।
cards.back: I am currently pursuing an MBA.

cards.front: मेरे पास Python, Java, और SQL का अच्छा knowledge है।
cards.back: I have good knowledge of Python, Java, and SQL. / I am proficient in Python, Java, and SQL.

cards.front: मैं self-motivated और goal-oriented person हूँ।
cards.back: I am a self-motivated and goal-oriented person.

cards.front: मेरी biggest weakness यह है कि मैं sometimes overthink करता हूँ।
cards.back: My biggest weakness is that I sometimes overthink.

cards.front: मुझे confident feel नहीं होता जब मैं English बोलता हूँ।
cards.back: I do not feel confident when I speak English.

cards.front: लेकिन मैं उस पर लगातार काम कर रहा हूँ।
cards.back: But I am consistently working on it.

cards.front: मेरी best quality यह है कि मैं हार नहीं मानता।
cards.back: My best quality is that I never give up.

cards.front: मुझे gardening का शौक है।
cards.back: I am fond of gardening. / I love gardening.

cards.front: मैं योग और meditation regularly करता हूँ।
cards.back: I practise yoga and meditation regularly.

cards.front: मुझे photography का जुनून है।
cards.back: I am passionate about photography.

cards.front: मेरे पास PMP certification है।
cards.back: I hold a PMP certification.

cards.front: मैं Bangalore में 5 साल से रह रहा हूँ।
cards.back: I have been living in Bangalore for 5 years.

cards.front: मैंने इस project में 40% cost savings लाई।
cards.back: I achieved 40% cost savings in this project.

cards.front: मैं अपने आप को एक lifelong learner मानता हूँ।
cards.back: I consider myself a lifelong learner.

cards.front: मुझे यह बताते हुए खुशी हो रही है कि मैंने recently award जीता।
cards.back: I am happy to share that I recently won an award.

cards.front: मैं cross-functional teams के साथ काम करने में अच्छा हूँ।
cards.back: I am good at working with cross-functional teams.

cards.front: मैं हमेशा अपना 100% देता हूँ।
cards.back: I always give my 100%.

cards.front: मेरे पास strong analytical skills हैं।
cards.back: I have strong analytical skills.

cards.front: मुझे comfortable feel होता है जब मैं अपने काम के बारे में बात करता हूँ।
cards.back: I feel comfortable talking about my work.

cards.front: मेरे supervisor मुझे detail-oriented कहते हैं।
cards.back: My supervisor calls me detail-oriented.

cards.front: मैं अपने career में next level तक पहुँचना चाहता हूँ।
cards.back: I want to reach the next level in my career.

cards.front: मुझे नए लोगों से मिलना और नई बातें सीखना पसंद है।
cards.back: I enjoy meeting new people and learning new things.

cards.front: मेरी company मुझे trust करती है complex projects handle करने के लिए।
cards.back: My company trusts me to handle complex projects.

cards.front: मुझे अपने काम से बहुत satisfaction मिलती है।
cards.back: I get a lot of satisfaction from my work.

cards.front: मैं English speaking course join करना चाहता हूँ।
cards.back: I want to join an English speaking course.

cards.front: मेरी English improve हो रही है।
cards.back: My English is improving.

cards.front: मुझे यकीन है कि मैं इस challenge को handle कर सकता हूँ।
cards.back: I am confident that I can handle this challenge.

cards.front: मैं अपनी skills को और sharpen करने के लिए कड़ी मेहनत कर रहा हूँ।
cards.back: I am working hard to sharpen my skills further.

cards.front: मेरे mentor ने मुझे बहुत support किया है।
cards.back: My mentor has supported me a lot.

cards.front: मैं अपनी life में balance रखना पसंद करता हूँ।
cards.back: I like to maintain a balance in my life.

cards.front: मुझे positive feedback मिलती रहती है।
cards.back: I keep getting positive feedback.

cards.front: मैं अपनी field में updated रहता हूँ।
cards.back: I stay updated in my field.

cards.front: मेरे पास strong work ethic है।
cards.back: I have a strong work ethic.

cards.front: मुझे अपने काम पर गर्व है।
cards.back: I am proud of my work.

cards.front: मैं हर दिन कुछ नया सीखने की कोशिश करता हूँ।
cards.back: I try to learn something new every day.

cards.front: मैं team के साथ effectively communicate कर सकता हूँ।
cards.back: I can communicate effectively with the team.

cards.front: मेरी life में family सबसे important है।
cards.back: Family is the most important thing in my life.

cards.front: मेरे पिता मुझे हमेशा inspire करते हैं।
cards.back: My father always inspires me.

cards.front: मैं अपने college के best students में से एक था।
cards.back: I was one of the best students in my college.

cards.front: मैं creative और innovative solutions find करना पसंद करता हूँ।
cards.back: I enjoy finding creative and innovative solutions.

cards.front: मुझे किसी भी topic पर confidently बोलने में कोई problem नहीं है।
cards.back: I have no problem speaking confidently on any topic.

cards.front: मैं professionally बहुत grow करना चाहता हूँ।
cards.back: I want to grow a lot professionally.

cards.front: मेरी wife एक doctor हैं और हम दोनों मिलकर काम करते हैं।
cards.back: My wife is a doctor and we both work together.

cards.front: मुझे नई responsibility लेना अच्छा लगता है।
cards.back: I enjoy taking on new responsibilities.

cards.front: मैं अपनी बात clearly express कर सकता हूँ।
cards.back: I can express my thoughts clearly.

cards.front: मुझे अपनी company बड़ी करनी है।
cards.back: I want to grow my company. / I want to expand my company.

cards.front: Find the error: 'Myself is Rahul Sharma, myself is from Delhi.'
cards.back: TWO errors: (1) 'Myself is' → 'My name is'. (2) 'myself is from' → 'I am from'. CORRECT: My name is Rahul Sharma. I am from Delhi.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am born in 1998 in Mumbai.'
cards.back: Error: 'am born' → 'was born'. CORRECT: I was born in 1998 in Mumbai.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am working since 3 years in this company.'
cards.back: TWO errors: (1) 'am working' should be 'have been working'. (2) 'since 3 years' should be 'for 3 years'. CORRECT: I have been working in this company for 3 years.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am doing job in bank from past 5 years.'
cards.back: THREE errors: (1) 'doing job' → 'working'. (2) 'in bank' → 'at a bank'. (3) 'from past 5 years' → 'for the past 5 years'. CORRECT: I have been working at a bank for the past 5 years.

cards.front: Find the error: 'My hobby are reading, cooking, and travel.'
cards.back: TWO errors: (1) 'hobby are' → 'hobbies are'. (2) 'travel' should be 'travelling' (parallel gerund form). CORRECT: My hobbies are reading, cooking, and travelling.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am liking to work in your company.'
cards.back: Error: 'am liking' → 'like'. Stative verb 'like' cannot be in continuous form. CORRECT: I like to work in your company. OR: I would like to work in your company.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am interested to music and painting.'
cards.back: Error: 'interested to' → 'interested in'. CORRECT: I am interested in music and painting.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I have joined this company in the year 2020.'
cards.back: Error: 'have joined' → 'joined'. Specific year (2020) requires Simple Past, not Present Perfect. CORRECT: I joined this company in 2020.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am a IT professional and a MNC employee.'
cards.back: TWO errors: (1) 'a IT' → 'an IT' (vowel sound). (2) 'a MNC' → 'an MNC' (vowel sound 'Em'). CORRECT: I am an IT professional and an MNC employee.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I passed out from Delhi University in 2019.'
cards.back: Error: 'passed out from' → 'graduated from'. 'Passed out' in standard English means 'fainted/lost consciousness'. CORRECT: I graduated from Delhi University in 2019.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I work in Google as a engineer.'
cards.back: TWO errors: (1) 'work in Google' → 'work at Google'. (2) 'a engineer' → 'an engineer' (vowel sound). CORRECT: I work at Google as an engineer.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am fond of to read and to cook.'
cards.back: Error: 'fond of to read' → 'fond of reading'. After preposition 'of', always use gerund. CORRECT: I am fond of reading and cooking.

cards.front: Find the error: 'My family is of 5 members. My brother is engineer.'
cards.back: TWO errors: (1) 'My family is of 5 members' → 'My family has 5 members' or 'There are 5 members in my family'. (2) 'My brother is engineer' → 'My brother is AN engineer'. CORRECT: My family has 5 members. My brother is an engineer.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am having 2 childrens and a lovely wife.'
cards.back: TWO errors: (1) 'am having' → 'have' (stative verb). (2) 'childrens' → 'children' (irregular plural). CORRECT: I have 2 children and a lovely wife.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I joined this company 2 years back.'
cards.back: Error: '2 years back' → '2 years ago'. 'Back' is informal Indian English; standard English uses 'ago'. CORRECT: I joined this company 2 years ago.

cards.front: Find the error: 'Nice to meet to you. I am very much excited.'
cards.back: TWO errors: (1) 'meet to you' → 'meet you'. (2) 'very much excited' → 'very excited'. CORRECT: Nice to meet you. I am very excited.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am knowing many people in this field.'
cards.back: Error: 'am knowing' → 'know'. Stative verb 'know' cannot be in continuous form. CORRECT: I know many people in this field.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I belong from Uttar Pradesh, India.'
cards.back: Error: 'belong from' → 'belong to'. Correct preposition after 'belong' is 'to', not 'from'. CORRECT: I belong to Uttar Pradesh, India.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am working as the software engineer at the Infosys.'
cards.back: TWO errors: (1) 'working as the software engineer' → 'working as a software engineer'. (2) 'at the Infosys' → 'at Infosys' (no 'the' before company names). CORRECT: I am working as a software engineer at Infosys.

cards.front: Find the error: 'My good name is Suresh. I am come from the Delhi.'
cards.back: THREE errors: (1) 'My good name is' → 'My name is'. (2) 'I am come from' → 'I come from' or 'I am from'. (3) 'the Delhi' → 'Delhi'. CORRECT: My name is Suresh. I am from Delhi.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am done my MBA from IIM Ahmedabad with 8.7 CGPA.'
cards.back: Error: 'am done' is wrong. Use 'completed' or 'have completed'. CORRECT: I completed my MBA from IIM Ahmedabad with 8.7 CGPA. OR: I have completed my MBA...

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am working since January 2022 in this organization.'
cards.back: Error: 'am working' → 'have been working'. For ongoing duration from past, use Present Perfect Continuous. CORRECT: I have been working in this organization since January 2022.

cards.front: Find the error: 'My sister is married since 2 years and she have 1 child.'
cards.back: THREE errors: (1) 'is married since 2 years' → 'has been married for 2 years'. (2) 'she have' → 'she has'. (3) '1 child' is fine but 'a child' is more natural. CORRECT: My sister has been married for 2 years and she has a child.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am a topper of my batch. I also did a course from a very reputed college.'
cards.back: Error: 'topper' is Indian English. Standard: 'I ranked first in my batch' OR 'I was the top student in my batch'. The second sentence is fine. CORRECT: I ranked first in my batch. I also completed a course from a reputed college.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I want to revert to your email as soon as possible.'
cards.back: Error: 'revert' is misused Indian English. In standard English, 'revert' means 'to return to a previous state'. Use 'reply' or 'respond'. CORRECT: I want to reply to your email as soon as possible.

cards.front: Find the error: 'Kindly do the needful and revert at the earliest.'
cards.back: THREE Indian English errors: (1) 'do the needful' → 'please take the necessary action'. (2) 'revert' → 'respond/reply'. (3) 'at the earliest' → 'as soon as possible'. CORRECT: Please take the necessary action and respond as soon as possible.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am a self-made person who never take help from others.'
cards.back: Error: 'never take' → 'never takes'. Subject 'I' with 'never' in present simple → the verb should agree with 'I': 'I never take'. CORRECT: I am a self-made person who never takes help from others. (Relative clause subject: 'who' = 'a self-made person' = singular = 'takes')

cards.front: Find the error: 'I have a wife and two childrens and we live in an apartment.'
cards.back: Error: 'childrens' → 'children'. Irregular plural: child → children (no 's' needed). CORRECT: I have a wife and two children and we live in an apartment.

cards.front: Find the error: 'My target is that I will become an manager in 2 years.'
cards.back: TWO errors: (1) 'an manager' → 'a manager' ('m' is consonant sound). (2) 'My target is that I will become' → 'My goal/target is to become'. CORRECT: My goal is to become a manager in 2 years.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am very much happy to join your organization.'
cards.back: Error: 'very much happy' → 'very happy' or 'so happy'. 'Very much' before verbs only, not adjectives. CORRECT: I am very happy to join your organization.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am looking to work in your esteemed organization from a very long time.'
cards.back: Error: 'from a very long time' → 'for a very long time'. Duration uses 'for', not 'from'. CORRECT: I have been looking to work in your esteemed organization for a very long time.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I have experienced in both product and service companies.'
cards.back: Error: 'I have experienced' → 'I have experience'. 'Experience' here is a noun. 'I have experienced' would mean 'I have undergone'. CORRECT: I have experience in both product and service companies.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am strong at communication and I good at teamwork.'
cards.back: Error: 'I good at teamwork' → 'I am good at teamwork'. Missing auxiliary verb 'am'. CORRECT: I am strong at communication and I am good at teamwork.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I work for an reputed organization in Pune.'
cards.back: Error: 'an reputed' → 'a reputed'. 'Reputed' starts with consonant sound 'r' → use 'a'. CORRECT: I work for a reputed organization in Pune.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am result-oriented professional with 8 years working experience.'
cards.back: TWO errors: (1) 'I am result-oriented professional' → 'I am a result-oriented professional' (missing article 'a'). (2) '8 years working experience' → '8 years of work experience'. CORRECT: I am a result-oriented professional with 8 years of work experience.

cards.front: Find the error: 'She is my good-friend who helps me a lot in my work.'
cards.back: Error: 'good-friend' — hyphen is wrong here. 'Good friend' is two separate words (no hyphen). CORRECT: She is my good friend who helps me a lot in my work.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I have done my post-graduation from Mumbai University.'
cards.back: No major grammar error here. However, 'post-graduation' is Indian English. More standard: 'postgraduate degree' or 'Master's degree'. BETTER: I completed my postgraduate degree from Mumbai University.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am currently working as the team leader in my company.'
cards.back: Small error: 'as the team leader' → 'as a team leader' (unless there is only one team leader, use 'a'). CORRECT: I am currently working as a team leader in my company.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I can communicate fluently in both English and in Hindi.'
cards.back: Minor error: The second 'in' is redundant. Better: 'I can communicate fluently in both English and Hindi'. CORRECT: I can communicate fluently in both English and Hindi.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I have been award Best Employee three times.'
cards.back: Error: 'have been award' → 'have been awarded' (passive = have been + past participle). CORRECT: I have been awarded Best Employee three times.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I take initiative and I loves challenges.'
cards.back: Error: 'I loves' → 'I love'. Subject 'I' takes base form of verb, not 's' form (that's for he/she/it). CORRECT: I take initiative and I love challenges.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am having total 5 years of experience.'
cards.back: Error: 'am having' → 'have'. Stative possession verb 'have' should not use continuous form. CORRECT: I have a total of 5 years of experience.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am in this company from last 3 years.'
cards.back: TWO errors: (1) 'am in' → 'have been in' (ongoing duration from past). (2) 'from last 3 years' → 'for the last 3 years'. CORRECT: I have been in this company for the last 3 years.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am wanting to learn from this opportunity.'
cards.back: Error: 'am wanting' → 'want'. Stative verb 'want' cannot be in continuous form. CORRECT: I want to learn from this opportunity.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am well experienced in field of marketing.'
cards.back: Error: 'in field of marketing' → 'in the field of marketing'. Article 'the' needed before 'field'. CORRECT: I am well-experienced in the field of marketing.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am from the Mumbai and I work at the Tata Consultancy Services.'
cards.back: TWO errors: (1) 'the Mumbai' → 'Mumbai' (no 'the' before city names). (2) 'the Tata Consultancy Services' → 'Tata Consultancy Services' (no 'the' before most company names). CORRECT: I am from Mumbai and I work at Tata Consultancy Services.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I completed my 12th with 88% marks from science stream.'
cards.back: Error: 'from science stream' → 'in the science stream'. 'Stream' takes 'in' and needs article 'the'. CORRECT: I completed my 12th with 88% marks in the science stream.

cards.front: Find the error: 'It is great pleasure for meeting you.'
cards.back: TWO errors: (1) 'great pleasure for' → 'great pleasure to' (or 'a great pleasure to'). (2) 'meeting you' after 'to' should be 'meet you' (infinitive). CORRECT: It is a great pleasure to meet you.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I look forwarding to work with your team.'
cards.back: TWO errors: (1) 'look forwarding' → 'look forward'. (2) 'to work' → 'to working' (after 'look forward to', use gerund). CORRECT: I look forward to working with your team.

cards.front: Find the error: 'Allow me for introducing myself to you all.'
cards.back: Error: 'allow me for introducing' → 'allow me to introduce'. The pattern is 'Allow me TO + base verb'. CORRECT: Allow me to introduce myself to you all.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am working at software domain from 4 years.'
cards.back: THREE errors: (1) 'working at software domain' → 'working in the software domain'. (2) 'am working' → 'have been working'. (3) 'from 4 years' → 'for 4 years'. CORRECT: I have been working in the software domain for 4 years.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am specializing in human resource management.'
cards.back: Error: 'am specializing' — while not completely wrong, 'specialize' is generally used in simple present for stable expertise. More natural: 'I specialize in human resource management.' BETTER: I specialize in human resource management.

cards.front: Find the error: 'My father has retired from government serviceman.'
cards.back: Error: 'government serviceman' → 'government service'. 'Serviceman' means military person. CORRECT: My father has retired from government service.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am very much confidence person who never gives up.'
cards.back: TWO errors: (1) 'very much confidence' → 'very confident' (adjective, not noun). (2) 'who never gives up' — grammatically fine but 'a confident person who' reads better. CORRECT: I am a very confident person who never gives up.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I have working experience of 3 years in finance domain.'
cards.back: Error: 'have working experience of 3 years' → 'have 3 years of work experience'. More natural phrasing. CORRECT: I have 3 years of work experience in the finance domain.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I completed my engineering in the year 2022 from NIT.'
cards.back: No major grammatical error, but 'in the year 2022' is slightly wordy. More natural: 'in 2022'. BETTER: I completed my engineering from NIT in 2022.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am very dedicated towards my work.'
cards.back: Minor error: 'dedicated towards' → 'dedicated to'. CORRECT: I am very dedicated to my work.

cards.front: Find the error: 'Thank you for giving me this golden chance.'
cards.back: No major error. However, 'golden chance' is informal/idiomatic. More professional: 'Thank you for this wonderful opportunity.' Either is acceptable depending on context.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I am passionate for learning new things.'
cards.back: Error: 'passionate for' → 'passionate about'. CORRECT: I am passionate about learning new things.

cards.front: Find the error: 'I had 5 years of experience in this field.'
cards.back: Error: 'had 5 years' implies the experience is now over/in the past. If you still have it, use 'have': 'I have 5 years of experience'. Use 'had' only if you no longer work in that field. CORRECT (if still working): I have 5 years of experience in this field.

modes.name: Learn Mode
modes.description: See front, guess, then flip to see answer

modes.name: Spaced Repetition
modes.description: Cards you miss appear more often
